disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Symptoms began weeks ago following a minor tibial fracture; tonight escalated from a dull ache to severe burning pain over ~24 hours — pain vastly out of proportion to the original injury
trigger_question_examples: When did this start and has anything happened to that leg recently?
Tell me about the injury — when did you break your leg and how has the pain changed since?
How does this pain compare to the pain from the original fracture?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: It all started after I broke my leg a few weeks ago. It was a dull ache while it was healing, but tonight it just went off — this burning is a hundred times worse than the break ever was.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Marked mechanical allodynia — light touch or a bedsheet on the dorsal foot provokes severe pain
trigger_question_examples: What makes the pain worse — does touching it or clothing set it off?
Can you tell me what happens when something light, like a sheet, touches the foot?
Is even gentle touch painful, or only pressure?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: Even the bedsheet touching it is unbearable. Just a light touch feels like it's burning or being cut — I can't have a sock or anything near it.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Unilateral vasomotor/sudomotor and trophic changes — oedema, shiny tight skin, mottled erythema, and altered sweating in the affected limb
trigger_question_examples: Has the colour, temperature, swelling or sweating of that leg changed compared to the other side?
Does the skin on the affected leg look or feel different — shiny, blotchy, sweaty?
Is there any change in how the skin looks or how much the leg sweats?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: Yeah — it's swollen and the skin has gone shiny and tight with red blotchy patches, and it seems to sweat differently to my other leg.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Reassuring pertinent negatives on patient-obtainable data — no fever or systemic illness; the foot is warm and normal-coloured (not pale/cold), sensation intact, and there is no spreading redness or red streaking
trigger_question_examples: Do you have a fever, or is the redness spreading up the leg with any red streaks?
Is the foot cold, pale, or numb, or is it still warm with normal colour and feeling?
Have you felt hot, shivery or unwell in yourself as well as the leg pain?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: No fever — I checked, it's 36.9 and I don't feel unwell in myself. The foot is still warm and its normal colour, I can feel it fine, and the redness isn't spreading or streaky.
disclosure_items.patient_deflection_template: I haven't really looked at that closely.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Maximum-dose paracetamol and ibuprofen have had zero analgesic effect
trigger_question_examples: What have you already taken for the pain and did it help at all?
Have you tried any painkillers, and what effect did they have?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: I've taken the maximum paracetamol and ibuprofen and it's done absolutely nothing — no relief at all.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Significant social/safety risk — lives alone, bedbound tonight, unable to weight-bear, high fall risk with a cluttered path to the bathroom
trigger_question_examples: Is there someone at home with you tonight, and how are you managing to move around safely?
I'm a bit worried about you being on your own — can you get to the bathroom safely, and is anyone able to check on you?
How are you coping at home with the leg like this — any risk of falling?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: I live on my own and I can't put any weight on the leg, so I'm stuck in bed. I'm honestly scared of falling trying to get to the bathroom — the room's cluttered and there's no one here.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: No chest pain or breathlessness — pertinent negative screening for pulmonary embolism as a complication of possible DVT
trigger_question_examples: Any chest pain, or shortness of breath, or coughing up blood?
Have you had any trouble breathing or any chest tightness alongside the leg?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: No, no chest pain and my breathing is fine — it's just the leg.
